preview.summary: A Group B clash between two experienced European sides built around veteran leaders. Switzerland arrive as one of the group favourites after a flawless qualifying campaign, organised and patient through midfield, while Bosnia rode dramatic playoff heroics to reach the finals and will back their physicality and set-piece threat. With co-hosts Canada and Qatar also in the group, points here matter early.
preview.homeForm: Won UEFA qualifying Group B unbeaten, four wins and two draws, conceding just twice; ranked around 19th.
preview.awayForm: Qualified via the playoffs, beating Italy on penalties in the final; ranked around 65th.
preview.homeKey: Granit Xhaka, captain at his fourth World Cup, dictates the midfield
preview.awayKey: Edin Dzeko, 40-year-old talisman and all-time top scorer
storylines: Xhaka's revival at Sunderland anchors a disciplined Swiss side
Dzeko defies age after powering Bosnia through the playoffs
Switzerland's control vs Bosnia's set-piece and aerial threat
preview.h2h: Rare meeting between the sides; Bosnia edged their only previous encounter.
